# netbox_custom_fields (Data Source)

Fetches a list of Custom Fields from Netbox.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# 全カスタムフィールドを一覧取得する例。
data "netbox_custom_fields" "all" {}

# カスタムフィールドの件数を出力する。
output "custom_field_count" {
  value = length(data.netbox_custom_fields.all.custom_fields)
}

# 最初のカスタムフィールド名を出力する。
output "first_custom_field_name" {
  value = length(data.netbox_custom_fields.all.custom_fields) > 0 ? data.netbox_custom_fields.all.custom_fields[0].name : "No custom fields found"
}

# dcim.device に割り当てられたカスタムフィールドのみをフィルタリングする例。
locals {
  device_custom_fields = [
    for cf in data.netbox_custom_fields.all.custom_fields :
    cf if contains(cf.content_types, "dcim.device")
  ]
}

output "device_custom_field_names" {
  value = [for cf in local.device_custom_fields : cf.name]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `custom_fields` (Attributes List) List of custom fields. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--custom_fields))
- `id` (String) Placeholder identifier for the data source.

<a id="nestedatt--custom_fields"></a>
### Nested Schema for `custom_fields`

Read-Only:

- `choices` (List of String) Available choices for select/multiselect fields.
- `content_types` (List of String) List of content types this custom field is assigned to.
- `description` (String) Description for the custom field.
- `filter_logic` (String) Filtering logic for this field.
- `group_name` (String) Custom fields within the same group will be displayed together.
- `id` (Number) The numeric ID of the custom field.
- `is_cloneable` (Boolean) Whether this field is copied when cloning an object.
- `label` (String) Display name for the field in the UI.
- `name` (String) Internal field name.
- `required` (Boolean) Whether a value is required for this field.
- `search_weight` (Number) Weighting for search.
- `type` (String) The type of the custom field.
- `ui_editable` (String) Controls editability in the UI.
- `ui_visible` (String) Controls visibility in the UI.
- `weight` (Number) Fields with higher weights appear lower in the form.
